name: ogr

metadata.name: ogr
metadata.summary: One API for multiple git forges.

documentation:
# One Git library to Rule

![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/ogr.svg)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/ogr.svg)
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/ogr.svg)
![PyPI - Status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/ogr.svg)
[![Build Status](https://zuul-ci.org/gated.svg)](https://softwarefactory-project.io/zuul/t/local/builds?project=packit-service/ogr)
[![black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![pre-commit](https://img.shields.io/badge/pre--commit-enabled-brightgreen?logo=pre-commit&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit)

Library for one API for many git forges. (e.g. GitHub, GitLab, Pagure).

## Currently supported git forges:

- GitHub
- GitLab
- Pagure

## To start using ogr:

See [Documentation of Index of Sub-modules, Functions & Classes](https://packit.github.io/ogr)

For examples of how to use `ogr` see [Jupyter examples](examples).

### GitHub

This snippet shows how to obtain all releases for certain GitHub project using ogr.

```python
from ogr.services.github import GithubService

service = GithubService(token="your_token")

ogr_project = service.get_project(
        repo="ogr",
        namespace="packit-service"
)

ogr_releases = ogr_project.get_releases()


for release in ogr_releases:
    print(release.tag_name)
```

This will output:

```
0.7.0
0.6.0
0.5.0
0.4.0
0.3.1
0.3.0
0.2.0
0.1.0
0.0.3
0.0.2
0.0.1
```

You can use the same API for other forges, you just need to replace `GithubService` with `PagureService`.

## Supported functionality

For more info on functionality that _is not_ supported in all services the same way
see [compatibility tables](COMPATIBILITY.md).

## Installation

On Fedora:

```
$ dnf install python3-ogr
```

You can also use our [`packit-releases` Copr repository](https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/packit/packit-releases/)
(contains also released versions of [OGR](https://github.com/packit/ogr)):

```
$ dnf copr enable packit/packit-releases
$ dnf install python3-ogr
```

Or from PyPI:

```
$ pip3 install --user ogr
```

You can also install OGR from the `main` branch, if you are brave enough:

You can use our [`packit-dev` Copr repository](https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/packit/packit-dev/)
(contains the latest development version of [ogr](https://github.com/packit/ogr)):

```
$ dnf copr enable packit/packit-dev
$ dnf install python3-ogr
```

Or

```
$ pip3 install --user git+https://github.com/packit/ogr.git
```

## Requirements

### Makefile

- [podman](https://github.com/containers/libpod)
- [ansible-bender](https://pypi.org/project/ansible-bender)

### Tests

Make sure to install prerequisite packages before first `make check`,
`make build`, or `make check-in-container`. See CONTRIBUTING.md for
details.

# Contribution notes

- Property should not connect to network.

# Contribution guidelines

For more info about contributing to our project see [our contribution guide](/CONTRIBUTING.md).

# Deprecation policy

For more info about deprecation policy see [Deprecation policy](https://github.com/packit/research/tree/main/deprecation)

modules.name: ogr.utils
documentation: Copyright Contributors to the Packit project.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: ogr.utils.filter_comments
documentation:
Filters comments from the given list.

Args:
    comments: List of comments to be filtered.
    filter_regex: Regex to be used for filtering body of the
        comments.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering by regex.
    reverse: Specifies ordering of the comments.

        Defaults to `False`, which means the order is kept from the input.
    author: Login of the author of the comments.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering by author.

Returns:
    List of comments that satisfy requested criteria.

functions.name: ogr.utils.search_in_comments
documentation:
Find match in pull request description or comments.

Args:
    comments: List of comments or bodies of comments
        to be searched through.
    filter_regex: Regex to be used for filtering with `re.search`.

Returns:
    Match that has been found, `None` otherwise.

functions.name: ogr.utils.filter_paths
documentation:
Filters paths from the given list.

Args:
    paths: List of paths to be filtered.
    filter_regex: Regex to be used for filtering paths.

Returns:
    List of path that satisfy regex.

functions.name: ogr.utils.indirect
documentation:
Decorator to wrap methods on `GitProject`s that call specialized classes.

Args:
    specialized_function: Static method of the specialized class
        that takes as first argument the `GitProject` itself.

Returns:
    Decorator that calls `specialized_function` once called.

    ok (bool): `True` if successful, `False` otherwise.

modules.name: ogr.parsing
documentation: Copyright Contributors to the Packit project.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: ogr.parsing.parse_git_repo
documentation:
Parses given URL of a git repository.

### Covered scenarios

1. URL in form: `www.domain.com/foo/bar`
    - with trailing `.git`
    - without `www.`
    - starting with `http://`, `https://`, `git://` or `git+https://`
2. URL in form: `git@domain.com:foo/bar`
    - with trailing `.git`
    - with `ssh://` at the start or with `git+ssh` instead of `git`
8. Pagure format of forks, e.g.
   `domain.com/fork/username/namespace/project`
9. Nested groups on GitLab or Pagure (empty namespace is supported as well)

Args:
    potential_url: URL of a git repository.

Returns:
    Object of RepoUrl class if can be parsed, `None` otherwise.

functions.name: ogr.parsing.get_username_from_git_url
documentation:
Returns username from the git URL.

Args:
    url: URL of the git repository.

Returns:
    Username if can be parsed, `None` otherwise.

functions.name: ogr.parsing.get_reponame_from_git_url
documentation:
Returns repository name from the git URL.

Args:
    url: URL of the git repository.

Returns:
    Repository name if can be parsed, `None` otherwise.

functions.name: ogr.parsing.strip_dot_git
documentation:
Strips `.git` from the given URL of a git repository.

Args:
    url: URL of the git repository.

Returns:
    URL without trailing `.git`.

classes.name: ogr.parsing.RepoUrl

methods.name: ogr.parsing.RepoUrl.get_instance_url
documentation:
Returns:
    Instance URL of host of the repository.

methods.name: ogr.parsing.RepoUrl.parse
documentation:
Details in `parse_git_repo` function.

Args:
    potential_url: URL of a git repository.

Returns:
    RepoUrl instance if can be parsed, `None` otherwise.

Class that represents repo URL.

Attributes:
    repo (str): Name of the repository.
    namespace (Optional[str]): Namespace of the repository, if has any.
    username (Optional[str]): Username of the repository owner, if can be
        specified.
    is_fork (bool): Flag denoting if repository is a fork, if can be
        specified (Pagure).
    hostname (Optional[str]): Hostname of host of the repository.
    scheme (Optional[str]): Protocol used to access repository.

modules.name: ogr.factory
documentation: Copyright Contributors to the Packit project.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: ogr.factory.use_for_service
documentation:
Class decorator that adds the class to the service mapping.

When the project url contains the `service` as a substring,
this implementation will be used to initialize the project.

When using this decorator, be sure that your class is initialized.
(Add the import to `ogr/__init__.py`)

Usage:
```py
@use_for_service("github.com")
class GithubService(BaseGitService):
    pass

@use_for_service("pagure.io")
@use_for_service("src.fedoraproject.org")
class PagureService(BaseGitService):
    pass
```

Args:
    service: URL of the service.

Returns:
    Decorator.

functions.name: ogr.factory.get_project
documentation:
Return the project for the given URL.

Args:
    url: URL of the project, e.g. `"https://github.com/packit/ogr"`.
    service_mapping_update: Custom mapping from
        service url/hostname (`str`) to service class.

        Defaults to no mapping.
    custom_instances: List of instances that will be
        used when creating a project instance.

        Defaults to `None`.
    force_custom_instance: Force picking a Git service from the
        `custom_instances` list, if there is any provided, raise an error if
        that is not possible.

        Defaults to `True`.
    **kwargs: Arguments forwarded to __init__ of the matching service.

Returns:
    `GitProject` using the matching implementation.

functions.name: ogr.factory.get_service_class_or_none
documentation:
Get the matching service class from the URL.

Args:
    url: URL of the project, e.g. `"https://github.com/packit/ogr"`.
    service_mapping_update: Custom mapping from service url/hostname (`str`) to service
        class.

        Defaults to `None`.

Returns:
    Matched class (subclass of `GitService`) or `None`.

functions.name: ogr.factory.get_service_class
documentation:
Get the matching service class from the URL.

Args:
    url: URL of the project, e.g. `"https://github.com/packit/ogr"`.
    service_mapping_update: Custom mapping from service url/hostname (str) to service
        class.

        Defaults to `None`.

Returns:
    Matched class (subclass of `GitService`).

functions.name: ogr.factory.get_instances_from_dict
documentation:
Load the service instances from the dictionary in the following form:

- `key`   : hostname, url or name that can be mapped to the service-type
- `value` : dictionary with arguments used when creating a new instance of the
service (passed to the `__init__` method)

e.g.:
```py
get_instances_from_dict({
    "github.com": {"token": "abcd"},
    "pagure": {
        "token": "abcd",
        "instance_url": "https://src.fedoraproject.org",
    },
}) == {
    GithubService(token="abcd"),
    PagureService(token="abcd", instance_url="https://src.fedoraproject.org")
}
```

When the mapping `key->service-type` is not recognised, you can add a `type`
key to the dictionary and specify the type of the instance.
(It can be either name, hostname or url. The used mapping is same as for
key->service-type.)

The provided `key` is used as an `instance_url` and passed to the `__init__`
method as well.

e.g.:
```py
get_instances_from_dict({
    "https://my.gtlb": {"token": "abcd", "type": "gitlab"},
}) == {GitlabService(token="abcd", instance_url="https://my.gtlb")}
```

Args:
    instances: Mapping from service name/url/hostname to attributes for the
        service creation.

Returns:
    Set of the service instances.

functions.name: ogr.read_only.log_output
documentation:
Logs output.

Args:
    text: Text message to be logged.
    default_prefix: Prefix of the log message.

        Defaults to `DEFAULT_RO_PREFIX_STRING`.
    namespace: Namespace where the message comes from.

        Defaults to `__name__`.

functions.name: ogr.read_only.if_readonly
documentation:
Decorator to log function and ovewrite return value of object methods.
Ignore function name as first parameter and ignore every other parameters.

Args:
    return_value: Returned value if given, `return_function`
        has higher priority if set.

        Defaults to `None`.
    return_function: Returned function with applies
        arguments and original caller.

        Defaults to `None`.
    log_message: String to be put to logger output.

        Defaults to `""`.

Returns:
    Any value that is expected to be returned from the function call or the
    specificied return value.

methods.name: ogr.services.gitlab.pull_request.GitlabPullRequest.create
documentation:
How to create PR:
-  upstream -> upstream - call on upstream, fork_username unset
-  fork -> upstream - call on fork, fork_username unset
   also can call on upstream with fork_username, not supported way of using
-  fork -> fork - call on fork, fork_username set
-  fork -> other_fork - call on fork, fork_username set to other_fork owner

methods.name: ogr.services.gitlab.pull_request.GitlabPullRequest.__get_fork
documentation:
Returns forked project of a requested user. Internal method, in case the fork
doesn't exist, raises GitlabAPIException.

Args:
    fork_username: Username of a user that owns requested fork.
    project: Project to search forks of.

Returns:
    Requested fork.

Raises:
    GitlabAPIException, in case the fork doesn't exist.

Access levels:

    20 => Reporter access
    30 => Developer access
    40 => Maintainer access
    50 => Owner access

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.service.PagureService.call_api
documentation:
Call API endpoint.

Args:
    url: URL to be called.
    method: Method of the HTTP request, e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`, etc.
    params: HTTP(S) query parameters in form of a dictionary.
    data: Data to be sent in form of a dictionary.

Returns:
    Dictionary representing response.

Raises:
    PagureAPIException, if error occurs.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.service.PagureService.call_api_raw
documentation:
Call API endpoint and returns raw response.

Args:
    url: URL to be called.
    method: Method of the HTTP request, e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`, etc.
    params: HTTP(S) query parameters in form of a dictionary.
    data: Data to be sent in form of a dictionary.

Returns:
    `RequestResponse` object that represents the response from the API
    endpoint.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.service.PagureService.get_raw_request
documentation:
Call API endpoint and wrap the response in `RequestResponse` type.

Args:
    url: URL to be called.
    method: Method of the HTTP request, e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`, etc.

        Defaults to `"GET"`.
    params: HTTP(S) query parameters in form of a dictionary.
    data: Data to be sent in form of a dictionary.
    header: Header of the HTTP request.

Returns:
    `RequestResponse` object representing the response.

Raises:
    ValueError, if JSON cannot be retrieved.

Get a URL from its parts.

Args:

    add_api_endpoint_part: Add part with API endpoint (`/api/0/`).

Returns:

modules.name: ogr.abstract
documentation: Copyright Contributors to the Packit project.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: ogr.abstract.__check_for_internal_failure
documentation:
Checks if exception is caused by internal failure from git forge.

Args:
    ex: Wrapped exception.

Raises:
    GitForgeInternalError, when exception was cause by an internal failure.
    APIException, exception itself when not an internal failure.

functions.name: ogr.abstract.__wrap_exception
documentation:
Wraps uncaught exception in one of ogr exceptions.

Args:
    ex: Unhandled exception from GitHub or GitLab.

Returns:
    Wrapped `ex` in respective `APIException`.

Raises:
    TypeError, when given unexpected type of exception.

functions.name: ogr.abstract.catch_common_exceptions
documentation:
Decorator catching common exceptions.

Args:
    function (Callable): Function or method to decorate.

Raises:
    GithubAPIException, if authentication to Github failed.
    GitlabAPIException, if authentication to Gitlab failed.
    OgrNetworkError, if network problems occurred while performing a request.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.CatchCommonErrors
documentation:
A metaclass wrapping methods with a common exception handler.

This handler catches exceptions which can occur almost anywhere
and catching them manually would be tedious and converts them
to an appropriate ogr exception for the user. This includes
exceptions such as:
    - authentication (from Github/Gitlab)
    - network errors

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue.create
documentation:
Open new issue.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the issue is to be opened.
    title: Title of the issue.
    body: Description of the issue.
    private: Is the new issue supposed to be confidential?

        **Supported only by GitLab and Pagure.**

        Defaults to unset.
    labels: List of labels that are to be added to
        the issue.

        Defaults to no labels.
    assignees: List of usernames of the assignees.

        Defaults to no assignees.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created issue.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue.get
documentation:
Get issue.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the issue is to be opened.
    issue_id: ID of the issue.

Returns:
    Object that represents requested issue.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue.get_list
documentation:
List of issues.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the issue is to be opened.
    status: Status of the issues that are to be
        included in the list.

        Defaults to `IssueStatus.open`.
    author: Username of the author of the issues.

        Defaults to no filtering by author.
    assignee: Username of the assignee on the issues.

        Defaults to no filtering by assignees.
    labels: Filter issues that have set specific labels.

        Defaults to no filtering by labels.

Returns:
    List of objects that represent requested issues.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue._get_all_comments
documentation:
Get list of all issue comments.

Returns:
    List of all comments on the issue.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue.get_comments
documentation:
Get list of issue comments.

Args:
    filter_regex: Filter the comments' content with `re.search`.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering.
    reverse: Whether the comments are to be returned in
        reversed order.

        Defaults to `False`.
    author: Filter the comments by author.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering.

Returns:
    List of issue comments.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Issue.can_close
documentation:
Check if user have permissions to modify an issue.

Args:

Returns:

classes.name: ogr.abstract.PRStatus
documentation: Enumeration that represents statuses of pull requests.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.CommitStatus
documentation: Enumeration that represents possible state of commit statuses.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.MergeCommitStatus
documentation: Enumeration that represents possible states of merge states of PR/MR.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.title
documentation: Title of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.id
documentation: ID of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.status
documentation: Status of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.url
documentation: Web URL of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.description
documentation: Description of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.author
documentation: Login of the author of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.source_branch
documentation: Name of the source branch (from which the changes are pulled).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.target_branch
documentation: Name of the target branch (where the changes are being merged).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.created
documentation: Datetime of creating the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.labels
documentation: Labels of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.diff_url
documentation: Web URL to the diff of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.patch
documentation: Patch of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.head_commit
documentation: Commit hash of the HEAD commit of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.target_branch_head_commit
documentation: Commit hash of the HEAD commit of the target branch.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.merge_commit_sha
documentation:
Commit hash of the merge commit of the pull request.

Before merging represents test merge commit, if git forge supports it.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.merge_commit_status
documentation: Current status of the test merge commit.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.source_project
documentation: Object that represents source project (from which the changes are pulled).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.target_project
documentation: Object that represents target project (where changes are merged).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.commits_url
documentation: Web URL to the list of commits in the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.closed_by
documentation: Login of the account that closed the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.create
documentation:
Create new pull request.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the pull request will be created.
    title: Title of the pull request.
    body: Description of the pull request.
    target_branch: Branch in the project where the changes are being
        merged.
    source_branch: Branch from which the changes are being pulled.
    fork_username: The username/namespace of the forked repository.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get
documentation:
Get pull request.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the pull request is located.
    id: ID of the pull request.

Returns:
    Object that represents pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get_list
documentation:
List of pull requests.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the pull requests are located.
    status: Filters out the pull requests.

        Defaults to `PRStatus.open`.

Returns:
    List of pull requests with requested status.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.update_info
documentation:
Update pull request information.

Args:
    title: The new title of the pull request.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no updating.
    description: The new description of the pull request.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no updating.

Returns:
    Pull request itself.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest._get_all_comments
documentation:
Get list of all pull request comments.

Returns:
    List of all comments on the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get_comments
documentation:
Get list of pull request comments.

Args:
    filter_regex: Filter the comments' content with `re.search`.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering.
    reverse: Whether the comments are to be returned in
        reversed order.

        Defaults to `False`.
    author: Filter the comments by author.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering.

Returns:
    List of pull request comments.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get_all_commits
documentation:
Returns:
    List of commit hashes of commits in pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.search
documentation:
Find match in pull request description or comments.

Args:
    filter_regex: Regex that is used to filter the comments' content with `re.search`.
    reverse: Reverse order of the comments.

        Defaults to `False`.
    description: Whether description is included in the search.

        Defaults to `True`.

Returns:
    `re.Match` if found, `None` otherwise.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.comment
documentation:
Add new comment to the pull request.

Args:
    body: Body of the comment.
    commit: Commit hash to which comment is related.

        Defaults to generic comment.
    filename: Path to the file to which comment is related.

        Defaults to no relation to the file.
    row: Line number to which the comment is related.

        Defaults to no relation to the line.

Returns:
    Newly created comment.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.close
documentation:
Close the pull request.

Returns:
    Pull request itself.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.merge
documentation:
Merge the pull request.

Returns:
    Pull request itself.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.add_label
documentation:
Add labels to the pull request.

Args:
    *labels: Labels to be added.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get_statuses
documentation:
Returns statuses for latest commit on pull request.

Returns:
    List of commit statuses of the latest commit.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.PullRequest.get_comment
documentation:
Returns a PR comment.

Args:
    comment_id: id of comment

Returns:
    Object representing a PR comment.

Attributes:
    project (GitProject): Project of the pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag._state_from_str
documentation:
Transforms state from string to enumeration.

Args:
    state: String representation of a state.

Returns:
    Commit status.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag._validate_state
documentation: Validates state of the commit status (if it can be used with forge).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag._from_raw_commit_flag
documentation: Sets attributes based on the raw flag that has been given through constructor.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag.get
documentation:
Acquire commit statuses for given commit in the project.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the commit is located.
    commit: Commit hash for which we request statuses.

Returns:
    List of commit statuses for the commit.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag.set
documentation:
Set a new commit status.

Args:
    project (GitProject): Project where the commit is located.
    commit: Commit hash for which we set status.
    state: State for the commit status.
    target_url: URL for the commit status.
    description: Description of the commit status.
    context: Identifier to group related commit statuses.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag.created
documentation: Datetime of creating the commit status.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.CommitFlag.edited
documentation: Datetime of editing the commit status.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.CommitComment
documentation:
Attributes:
    sha (str): Hash of the related commit.
    body (str): Body of the comment.
    author (str): Login of the author.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.GitTag
documentation:
Class representing a git tag.

Attributes:
    name (str): Name of the tag.
    commit_sha (str): Commit hash of the tag.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.AccessLevel
documentation:
Enumeration representing an access level to the repository.

| Value from enumeration | GitHub   | GitLab                  | Pagure |
| ---------------------- | -------- | ----------------------- | ------ |
| `AccessLevel.pull`     | pull     | guest                   | ticket |
| `AccessLevel.triage`   | triage   | reporter                | ticket |
| `AccessLevel.push`     | push     | developer               | commit |
| `AccessLevel.admin`    | admin    | maintainer              | commit |
| `AccessLevel.maintain` | maintain | owner (only for groups) | admin  |

classes.name: ogr.abstract.Release

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.title
documentation: Title of the release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.body
documentation: Body of the release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.git_tag
documentation: Object that represents tag tied to the release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.tag_name
documentation: Tag tied to the release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.url
documentation: URL of the release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.created_at
documentation: Datetime of creating the release.
TODO: Check if should really be string

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.tarball_url
documentation: URL of the tarball.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.get
documentation:
Get a single release.

Args:
    identifier: Identifier of the release.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.
    name: Name of the release.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.
    tag_name: Tag that the release is tied to.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.

Returns:
    Object that represents release that satisfies requested condition.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.get_latest
documentation:
Returns:
    Object that represents the latest release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.get_list
documentation:
Returns:
    List of the objects that represent releases.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.create
documentation:
Create new release.

Args:
    project: Project where the release is to be created.
    tag: Tag which is the release based off.
    name: Name of the release.
    message: Message or description of the release.
    ref: Git reference, mainly commit hash for the release. If provided
        git tag is created prior to creating a release.

        Defaults to `None`.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.save_archive
documentation:
Save tarball of the release to requested `filename`.

Args:
    filename: Path to the file to save archive to.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.Release.edit_release
documentation:
Edit name and message of a release.

Args:
    name: Name of the release.
    message: Description of the release.

Object that represents release.

Attributes:
    project (GitProject): Project on which the release is created.

classes.name: ogr.abstract.GitService

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.get_project
documentation:
Get the requested project.

Args:
    namespace (str): Namespace of the project.
    user (str): Username of the project's owner.
    repo (str): Repository name.

Returns:
    Object that represents git project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.get_project_from_url
documentation:
Args:
    url: URL of the git repository.

Returns:
    Object that represents project from the parsed URL.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.hostname
documentation: Hostname of the service.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.user
documentation: User authenticated through the service.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.change_token
documentation:
Change an API token. Only for the current instance and newly created projects.

Args:
    new_token: New token to be set.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.set_auth_method
documentation:
Override the default auth method.
Can be used when the service has more auth methods available.

Args:
    method: the method identifier (a str name)

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.reset_auth_method
documentation: Set the auth method to the default one.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.project_create
documentation:
Create new project.

Args:
    repo: Name of the newly created project.
    namespace: Namespace of the newly created project.

        Defaults to currently authenticated user.
    description: Description of the newly created project.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.list_projects
documentation:
List projects for given criteria.

Args:
    namespace: Namespace to list projects from.
    user: Login of the owner of the projects.
    search_pattern: Regular expression that repository name should match.
    language: Language to be present in the project, e.g. `"python"` or
        `"html"`.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitService.get_group
documentation: Get a group by name.

Attributes:
    instance_url (str): URL of the git forge instance.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    repo: Name of the project.
    service: GitService instance.
    namespace: Namespace of the project.

        - GitHub: username or org name.
        - GitLab: username or org name.
        - Pagure: namespace (e.g. `"rpms"`).

          In case of forks: `"fork/{username}/{namespace}"`.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.description
documentation:
Returns:
    Project description.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.description
documentation:
Args:
    new_description: description to set for project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.delete
documentation: Delete the project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.exists
documentation:
Check the existence of the repo.

Returns:
    `True` if the project exists, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.is_private
documentation:
Is this repository private (accessible only by users with permissions).

Returns:
    `True`, if the repository is private.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.is_forked
documentation:
Is this repository forked by the authenticated user?

Returns:
    `True`, if the repository is fork.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.is_fork
documentation: `True` if the project is a fork.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.full_repo_name
documentation: Get repo name with namespace, e.g. `rpms/python-docker-py`.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.parent
documentation: Parent project if the project is a fork, otherwise `None`.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.has_issues
documentation: `True` if issues are enabled on the project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_branches
documentation:
Returns:
    List with names of branches in the project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.default_branch
documentation: Default branch (usually `main`, `master` or `trunk`).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_commits
documentation:
Get list of commits for the project.

Args:
    ref: Ref to start listing commits from, defaults to the default project branch.

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_description
documentation:
Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_fork
documentation:
Provide GitProject instance of a fork of this project.

Args:
    create: Create fork if it does not exist.

Returns:
    `None` if the project is fork itself or there is no fork, otherwise

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_owners
documentation:
Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.who_can_close_issue

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.who_can_merge_pr

Args:

Returns:

documentation:
Args:

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.add_user
documentation:
Add user to project.

documentation:
Remove user from project.

Args:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.request_access
documentation:
Request an access to the project (cannot specify access level to be granted;
needs to be approved and specified by the user with maintainer/admin rights).

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.add_group
documentation:
Add group to project.

Args:

    access_level: Permissions for the group.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.remove_group
documentation:
Remove group from project.

Args:
    group: Name of the group.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_issue_list
documentation:
List of issues.

Args:
    status: Status of the issues that are to be
        included in the list.

        Defaults to `IssueStatus.open`.
    author: Username of the author of the issues.

        Defaults to no filtering by author.
    assignee: Username of the assignee on the issues.

        Defaults to no filtering by assignees.
    labels: Filter issues that have set specific labels.

        Defaults to no filtering by labels.

Returns:
    List of objects that represent requested issues.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_issue
documentation:
Get issue.

Args:
    issue_id: ID of the issue.

Returns:
    Object that represents requested issue.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_issue_info
documentation:
Get issue info.

Args:
    issue_id: ID of the issue.

Returns:
    Object that represents requested issue.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.create_issue
documentation:
Open new issue.

Args:
    title: Title of the issue.
    body: Description of the issue.
    private: Is the new issue supposed to be confidential?

        **Supported only by GitLab and Pagure.**

        Defaults to unset.
    labels: List of labels that are to be added to
        the issue.

        Defaults to no labels.
    assignees: List of usernames of the assignees.

        Defaults to no assignees.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created issue.

Raises:
    IssueTrackerDisabled, if issue tracker is disabled.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_pr_list
documentation:
List of pull requests.

Args:
    status: Status of the pull requests that are to be included in the list.

        Defaults to `PRStatus.open`.

Returns:
    List of objects that represent pull requests with requested status.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_pr
documentation:
Get pull request.

Args:
    pr_id: ID of the pull request.

Returns:
    Object that represents requested pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_pr_files_diff
documentation:
Get files diff of a pull request.

Args:
    pr_id: ID of the pull request.

Returns:
    Dictionary representing files diff.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_tags
documentation:
Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_sha_from_tag
documentation:
Args:

Returns:
    Commit hash of the commit from the requested tag.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_release
documentation:
Get a single release.

Args:
    identifier: Identifier of the release.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.
    name: Name of the release.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.
    tag_name: Tag that the release is tied to.

        Defaults to `None`, which means not being used.

Returns:
    Object that represents release that satisfies requested condition.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_latest_release
documentation:
Returns:
    Object that represents the latest release.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_releases
documentation:
Returns:
    List of the objects that represent releases.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.create_release
documentation:
Create new release.

Args:
    tag: Tag which is the release based off.
    name: Name of the release.
    message: Message or description of the release.
    ref: Git reference, mainly commit hash for the release. If provided

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.create_pr
documentation:
Create new pull request.

Args:
    title: Title of the pull request.
    body: Description of the pull request.
    target_branch: Name of the branch where the changes are merged.
    source_branch: Name of the branch from which the changes are pulled.
    fork_username: The username of forked repository.

        Defaults to `None`.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created pull request.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.commit_comment
documentation:
Add new comment to a commit.

Args:
    commit: Hash of the commit.
    body: Body of the comment.
    filename: Name of the file that is related to the comment.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no relation to file.
    row: Number of the row that the comment is related to.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no relation to the row.

Returns:
    Object that represents newly created commit comment.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_commit_comments
documentation:
Get comments for a commit.

Args:
    commit: The hash of the commit.

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_commit_comment
documentation:
Get commit comment.

Args:
    commit_sha: SHA of the commit
    comment_id: ID of the commit comment

Returns:

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.set_commit_status
documentation:
Create a status on a commit.

Args:
    commit: The hash of the commit.
    state: The state of the status.
    target_url: The target URL to associate with this status.
    description: A short description of the status.
    context: A label to differentiate this status from the status of other systems.
    trim: Whether to trim the description to 140 characters.

        Defaults to `False`.

Returns:
    Object that represents created commit status.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_commit_statuses
documentation:
Get statuses of the commit.

Args:
    commit: Hash of the commit.

Returns:
    List of all commit statuses on the commit.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_git_urls
documentation:
Get git URLs for the project.

Returns:
    Dictionary with at least SSH and HTTP URLs for the current project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.fork_create
documentation:
Fork this project using the authenticated user.

Args:
    namespace: Namespace where the project should be forked.

        Defaults to `None`, which means forking to the namespace of
        currently authenticated user.

Returns:
    Fork of the current project.

Raises:
    In case the fork already exists.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.change_token
documentation:
Change an API token. Only for the current instance.

Args:
    new_token: New token to be set.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_file_content
documentation:
Get a content of the file in the repo.

Args:
    path: Path to the file.
    ref: Branch or commit.

        Defaults to repo's default branch.

Returns:
    Contents of the file as string.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: if there is no such file.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_files
documentation:
Get a list of file paths of the repo.

Args:
    ref: Branch or commit.

        Defaults to repo's default branch.
    filter_regex: Filter the paths with `re.search`.

        Defaults to `None`, which means no filtering.
    recursive: Whether to return only top directory files
        or all files recursively.

        Defaults to `False`, which means only top-level directory.

Returns:
    List of paths of the files in the repo.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_forks
documentation:
Returns:
    All forks of the project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_web_url
documentation:
Returns:
    Web URL of the project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_sha_from_branch
documentation:
Returns:
    Commit SHA of head of the branch. `None` if no branch was found.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.get_contributors
documentation:
Returns:
    Set of all contributors to the given project.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.users_with_write_access
documentation:
Returns:
    List of users who have write access to the project

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitProject.has_write_access
documentation:
Decides whether a given user has write access to the project.

Args:
    user: The user we are going to check to see if he/she has access

Returns:
    Login of the user.

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitUser.get_email

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitUser.get_projects

methods.name: ogr.abstract.GitUser.get_forks
documentation:
Returns:

Represents currently authenticated user through service.

Represents labels on PRs and issues.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.pull_request.PagurePullRequest.get_files_diff
documentation:
Retrieve pull request diff statistics.

Pagure API tends to return ENOPRSTATS error when a pull request is transitioning
from open to other states, so you can use `retries` and `wait_seconds` to try to
mitigate that.


Args:
    project: Pagure project.
    pr_id: Pull request ID.
    retries: Number of extra attempts.
    wait_seconds: Delay between attempts.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.pull_request.PagurePullRequest.set_flag
documentation:
Set a flag on a pull-request to display results or status of CI tasks.

See "Flag a pull-request" at https://pagure.io/api/0/#pull_requests-tab
for a full description of the parameters.

Args:
    username: The name of the application to be presented to users
        on the pull request page.
    comment: A short message summarizing the presented results.
    url: A URL to the result of this flag.
    status: The status to be displayed for this flag.
    percent: A percentage of completion compared to the goal.
    uid: A unique identifier used to identify a flag on the pull-request.

Returns:
    Dictionary with the response received from Pagure.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._call_project_api
documentation:
Call project API endpoint.

Args:
    *args: String parts of the URL, e.g. `"a", "b"` will call `project/a/b`
    add_fork_part: If the project is a fork, use `fork/username` prefix.

        Defaults to `True`.
    add_api_endpoint_part: Add part with API endpoint (`/api/0/`).

        Defaults to `True`.
    method: Method of the HTTP request, e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`, etc.
    params: HTTP(S) query parameters in form of a dictionary.
    data: Data to be sent in form of a dictionary.

Returns:
    Dictionary representing response.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._call_project_api_raw
documentation:
Call project API endpoint.

Args:
    *args: String parts of the URL, e.g. `"a", "b"` will call `project/a/b`
    add_fork_part: If the project is a fork, use `fork/username` prefix.

        Defaults to `True`.
    add_api_endpoint_part: Add part with API endpoint (`/api/0/`).

        Defaults to `True`.
    method: Method of the HTTP request, e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`, etc.
    params: HTTP(S) query parameters in form of a dictionary.
    data: Data to be sent in form of a dictionary.

Returns:
    `RequestResponse` object containing response.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._get_users_with_given_access
documentation:
Get all users (considering groups) with the access levels given by list.

Arguments:
    access_levels: list of access levels, e.g. ['commit', 'admin']

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._get_entity_accounts_with_access
documentation:
Get the entity account names (users or groups) with the access levels given by the set.

Arguments:
    access_levels: list of access levels, e.g. ['commit', 'admin']
    entity_type: 'users' or 'groups'

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._get_user_accounts_with_access
documentation: Get the users with the access levels given by the set.

methods.name: ogr.services.pagure.project.PagureProject._get_group_accounts_with_access
documentation: Get the groups with the access levels given by list.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.service.GithubService.__init__
documentation:
If multiple authentication methods are provided, they are prioritised:
    1. Tokman
    2. GithubApp
    3. TokenAuthentication (which is also default one, that works without specified token)

methods.name: ogr.services.github.pull_request.GithubPullRequest.create
documentation:
The default behavior is the pull request is made to the immediate parent repository
if the repository is a forked repository.
If you want to create a pull request to the forked repo, please pass
the `fork_username` parameter.

functions.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.value_or_NotSet
documentation:
Wrapper for PyGithub, allows us to transform `None` into PyGithub's `NotSet`.

Args:
    value: Value that can be None.

Returns:
    If value is not None, value is returned; NotSet otherwise.

functions.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.create_github_check_run_output
documentation:
Helper function for constructing valid GitHub output for check run.

Args:
    title: Title of the output.
    summary: Summary of the output.
    text: Optional text for the output. Can be markdown formatted.

        Defaults to `None`.
    annotations: Optional annotations that are tied to source code.

Returns:
    Dictionary that represents valid output for check run.

classes.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRunStatus
documentation: Represents statuses GitHub check run can have.

classes.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRunResult
documentation: Represents conclusion/result of the GitHub check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.name
documentation: Name of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.commit_sha
documentation: Commit SHA that check run is related to.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.url
documentation: URL with additional details.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.external_id
documentation: External ID that can be used internally by the integrator.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.status
documentation: Current status of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.started_at
documentation: Timestamp of start of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.conclusion
documentation: Conclusion/result of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.completed_at
documentation: Timestamp of completion of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.output
documentation: Output of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.app
documentation: Github App of the check run.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.change_status
documentation:
Changes the status of the check run and checks the validity of new state.

Args:
    status: Status of the check run to be set. If set to completed, you
        must provide conclusion.

        Defaults to `None`.
    completed_at: Timestamp of completion of the check run. If set, you
        must provide conclusion.

        Defaults to `None`.
    conclusion: Conclusion/result of the check run. If only conclusion
        is set, status is automatically set to completed.

        Defaults to `None`.

Raises:
    OperationNotSupported, if given completed or timestamp of completed
        without conclusion.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.get_list
documentation:
Returns list of GitHub check runs.

Args:
    project: Project from which the check runs are retrieved.
    commit_sha: Commit to which are the check runs related to.
    name: Name of the check run for filtering.

        Defaults to `None`, no filtering.
    status: Status of the check runs to be returned.

        Defaults to `None`, no filtering.

Returns:
    List of the check runs.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.get
documentation:
Retrieves GitHub check run as ogr object.

Args:
    project: Project from which the check run is retrieved.
    check_run_id: Check run ID.

        Defaults to `None`, i.e. is not used for query.
    commit_sha: Commit SHA from which the check run is to be retrieved.
        If set, returns latest check run for the commit.

        Defaults to `None`, i.e. is not used for query.

Returns:
    GithubCheckRun object or `None` if no check run is found.

Raises:
    OperationNotSupported, in case there is no parameter for query set
        or both are set.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.check_run.GithubCheckRun.create
documentation:
Creates new check run.

Args:
    project: Project where the check run is to be created.
    name: Name of the check run.
    commit_sha: Hash of the commit that check run is related to.
    url: URL with details of the run.

        Defaults to `None`.
    external_id: External ID that can be used internally by integrator.

        Defaults to `None`.
    status: Status of the check run.

        Defaults to queued.
    started_at: Timestamp of starting the check run.

        Defaults to `None`.
    conclusion: Conclusion of the check run. Should be set with status
        completed.

        Defaults to `None`.
    completed_at: Timestamp of completion of the check run. If set, you
        must provide conclusion.

        Defaults to `None`.
    output: Output of the check run.
    actions: List of possible follow-up actions for the check run.

Returns:
    Created check run object.

Raises:
    OperationNotSupported, if given completed status or completion
        timestamp and no conclusion.

Returns:

documentation:
Returns:
    Generic PyGithub instance. Used for `GitUser` for example.

methods.name: ogr.services.github.auth_providers.abstract.GithubAuthentication.try_create
documentation:
Tries to construct authentication object from provided keyword arguments.

Returns:
    `GithubAuthentication` object or `None` if the creation was not
    successful.
Represents a token manager for authentication via GitHub App.
